LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 17, 2013 TO BE HEARD WITH DEFENDANT'S OTHER MOTIONS TO QUASH. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT WAS PERSONALLY SERVED ON JULY 7, 2013. THE RETURN OF THE PROCESS SERVER ESTABLISHES A PRESUMPTION, AFFECTING THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. EVIDENCE CODE 647. HOWEVER, HERE, DEFENDANT DECLARES UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY THAT SHE WAS NOT HOME FROM JULY 6 TO JULY 14, 2013 DURING WHICH TIME THE SUMMONS WAS ALLEGEDLY SERVED ON HER PERSONALLY. IT IS NOT CLEAR WHETHER THE PROOF OF SERVICE WAS SIGNED (THE COPY ATTACHED TO THE OPPOSITION HAS ONLY "S" ON THE SIGNATURE LINE). THERE IS NO DECLARATION FROM THE PROCESS SERVER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. (CCP 430.10(E).) MS. POLAKOVIC HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE VIOLATIONS OF THE FAIR DEBT COLLECTION PRACTICES ACT (FDCPA) AND THE ROSENTHAL DEBT COLLECTION PRACTICES ACT. THE ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT CLEARLY BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ON THE FACE OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S OTHER ARGUMENTS INVOLVE QUESTIONS OF FACT, THAT ARE NOT SUBJECT TO DEMURRER. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT DID NOT CITE TO ANY CALIFORNIA LEGAL AUTHORITY IN SUPPORT OF ITS REQUEST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES A . MICHEL, TEL. NO. 239-0156 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled. (CCP 430.10(e).) Ms. Polakovic has pled sufficient facts to allege violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) and the Rosenthal Debt Collection Practices Act. The allegations are not clearly barred by the statute of limitations on the face of the Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's other arguments involve questions of fact, that are not subject to demurrer. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. Cross-Defendant did not cite to any California legal authority in support of its request. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James A. Michel, Esq. of the Law Offices of James A. Michel, Tel. 415.951.4949, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is granted. Plaintiff fails to meet its burden to show that Defendant was personally served on July 7, 2013. The return of the process server establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence. Evidence Code 647. However, here, Defendant declares under penalty of perjury that she was not home from July 6 to July 14, 2013 during which time the summons was allegedly served on her personally. It is not clear whether the proof of service was signed (the copy attached to the opposition has only "S" on the signature line). There is no declaration from the process server. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
